required_skills: Gameplay systems programming in C++ (Unreal Engine) or C# (Unity) at a professional studio level
Character movement and controller programming including locomotion, jumping, wall-running, and physics-based character interactions
Combat system design and implementation including hit detection, damage models, ability systems, and combo mechanics
Input system programming for multi-platform support (keyboard/mouse, gamepad, touch) with input buffering and rebinding
Game Feel engineering including camera shake, controller rumble, hit-stop, and animation-driven responsiveness
Profiling and optimization of CPU-intensive gameplay code including tick management and object pooling
Collaboration with game designers through rapid prototyping, iteration cycles, and GDD implementation
Animation system integration including state machines, blend trees, and procedural animation in engine
preferred_skills: Ability system frameworks such as Unreal's Gameplay Ability System (GAS) for complex skill and status effect pipelines
Networked multiplayer gameplay programming with client-side prediction, lag compensation, and server reconciliation
Procedural animation techniques including inverse kinematics (IK) and motion matching for character realism
Vehicle physics programming including suspension systems, drivetrain simulation, and terrain interaction
Data-driven design approaches using scriptable objects (Unity) or data assets (Unreal) for designer-friendly tuning
VR/AR gameplay interaction programming for immersive input modalities (hand tracking, gaze, spatial controllers)
Experience contributing to a shipped AAA or AA title in a gameplay programming role
responsibilities: Implement and iterate on core gameplay mechanics including player movement, combat, abilities, and interaction systems in C++ or C# with a focus on responsiveness and game feel
Develop character controller systems that handle locomotion, state transitions, and physics interactions across varied terrain and environmental conditions
Build and maintain combat systems encompassing melee and ranged attack pipelines, hit registration, damage calculation, and status effect management
Partner closely with game designers to rapidly prototype new gameplay features, gathering playtesting feedback and refining implementations within agile sprint cycles
Integrate animation state machines and blend trees within the engine to produce fluid, context-aware character animations driven by gameplay state
Implement multi-platform input systems supporting keyboard, mouse, gamepad, and touch with support for input remapping and accessibility options
Profile gameplay code using Unreal Insights or Unity Profiler, identifying and resolving CPU bottlenecks that impact frame rate on target hardware
Design and implement data-driven tuning systems using scriptable objects or data assets that empower designers to adjust gameplay parameters without engineering support
Develop and maintain the ability and progression systems including skill trees, XP models, unlockable perks, and cooldown management
Write comprehensive technical documentation for gameplay systems, ensuring knowledge transfer across engineering and design teams
